danger_signals.visual_form: grip loss on track surface, visual spinning/loss of control during cornering
danger_signals.meaning: Qualifying tires degrade rapidly; player cannot drive more than a couple laps without losing grip and spinning out on nearly every corner

danger_signals.visual_form: car contact with visible track walls/obstacles
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of race position, potential race retirement

danger_signals.visual_form: ideal line visualization on tarmac; deviation from line shown visually
danger_signals.meaning: Suboptimal corner speed, loss of time to competitors

safety_signals.visual_form: line displayed on tarmac surface showing optimal path through corners
safety_signals.meaning: Guidance for safe, fast racing trajectory; learning aid for track layout
Safe corner entry; player assisted to brake at correct moment

safety_signals.visual_form: cars visually steering slightly into corners autonomously when activated
safety_signals.meaning: Compensation for digital controller limitations; vehicle stability assistance

safety_signals.visual_form: prevention of wheel spin animation at acceleration
safety_signals.meaning: Traction control; safe acceleration out of corners

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: team logos and driver helmets represented 1991 season designs
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: pit crew uniforms, team garage visual identity, car body paint schemes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 16 accurately modelled Formula One tracks; player car positioned on track relative to competitors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: AI drivers compete for track position; tire wear affects all competitors' handling progressively

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: special qualifying tire compound available; same track but empty of other competitors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: qualifying tires offer higher grip initially but degrade extremely rapidly (couple laps maximum); single-lap focus

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: ideal racing line visible on track; optional UI overlays for brake points, gear suggestions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player car responds with assistance smoothing; no penalty for mistakes; allows skill progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: no visual assists; raw track representation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: realistic physics; tire wear modeled throughout race; 1/1000 second chronometer becomes meaningful; races won/lost by thousandths of seconds

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: camera automatically adjusts position and angle based on on-track action; multiple cinematic angles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: replay system autonomously changes framing to show relevant racing events

environmental_storytelling.example: player can recognize their physical location on real-life circuits; 16 tracks replicate actual Formula One venues with sufficient detail for spatial recognition
gear ratios, tyre compounds, and wing settings visually manifest as different car handling characteristics; player observes how corner entry speed and acceleration angle affect trajectory
tire wear visible through progressive grip loss; tires that were fresh show controlled handling, worn tires show unpredictable spinning behavior
team colors, logos, and garage designs establish faction/team identity independent of narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: predictable handling; responds precisely to steering and throttle input; can brake late and accelerate early
high-grip initially then rapid degradation; spinning out on nearly every corner after 2 laps; loss of control on acceleration
applies brakes autonomously at correct corner entry timing; smooths deceleration curve
steers slightly into corners autonomously; compensates for digital controller on-off nature; provides subtle vehicle control
compete for track position; affected by tire wear; respond to player car proximity

notes: Formula One Grand Prix is a non-licensed F1 simulation that uses fictional driver names but accurate team liveries and helmet designs from the 1991 season. The game's visual communication strategy centers on physics manifestation (tire wear, handling changes) rather than narrative elements. Safety signals are primarily UI-based teaching tools. The 'world state to visual' mapping emphasizes the simulation's layered difficulty system where visual assists change based on player mode selection. No faction conflict or political narrative exists; the game purely simulates competitive racing scenarios.
